statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, write a function to detect if all subarrays have a unique length. If not, return the first subarray with a repeating length. Otherwise, return an empty array.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers where each element is between 1 and 10^9.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an array of integers if a subarray with repeating length is found, otherwise, return an empty array.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input array is non-empty and all elements are within the specified range.

editorial.title: Correcting Inaccurate Subarray Length Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to use prefix sum to calculate the cumulative sum of the array.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a brute force approach to calculate the length of each subarray and count the occurrences of each length.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use prefix sum to calculate the cumulative sum of the array, then use two pointers to find the first subarray with a repeating length.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct approach is to use prefix sum to calculate the cumulative sum of the array, and then find the first subarray with a repeating length.
